name: Bhai Vishan Shah Singh
body: The information below regarding Bhai Vishan Shah Singh Ji was written by his son, Harmeet Shah Singh, a renowned journalist.

Born in 1927 at Dera Ismail Khan in what is now in Pakistan, Bhai Sahib Bhai Vishan Shah Singh Ji moved to East Punjab in 1947. In the early years after the partition, he spent time in Sri Amritsar Sahib, listening to Kirtan at Sri Darbar Sahib. With a gifted melodious voice, sur and devotion, he imbibed Kirtan singing by the grace of Guru Ramdas Patshah.

The Shah family he was born in has its own rich history linked to the Guru period. Bhai Soma Ji, then a teen, would donate all his daily earnings from selling snacks towards the sewa of the holy sarovar. Guru Ramdas Patshah blessed Bhai Soma with the title Shah. During Guru Gobind Singh Sahib’s period, Bhai Soma Shah Ji’s descendent, Bhai Mehar Shah, took amrit and Guru Sahib asked him to retain Shah as his middle name and add Singh as his last. Since then, the male members of the Shah family in the Sikh community use Shah as their middle and Singh as their last names.
Despite his immense talent, Bhai Vishan Shah Singh Ji never ran after fame. Rather, Bhai Vishan Shah Singh Ji avoided it.

Bhai Vishan Shah Singh Ji passed in 2000 from a heart condition, but his voice lives with us, rendering the sublime and immortal Guru ka Kirtan in a unique style. Depth, clarity, sur, and above all, the slowness that helps transport the listening soul up to the Divine in a natural rhythm.
